domainDetection.reasoning: The document discusses customer adoption rates, customer satisfaction metrics (NPS, CSAT), onboarding challenges, the risk of non-renewal, and the need for an executive review, all of which are key indicators and responsibilities within Customer Success.

executionPlanReasoning: Fallback planner selected agents using deterministic rules.

evidence: Customer stated they may not renew.

retrievedKnowledge.title: Executive Business Review (EBR) Playbook
retrievedKnowledge.content: When a customer exhibits high churn risk signals (NPS < 5, adoption < 40%, or explicit renewal concerns), initiate an Executive Business Review within 7 days. The EBR should include the customer's executive sponsor and your internal VP of Customer Success. Agenda: review ROI achieved, address top concerns, present renewal roadmap. Prepare a slide deck with usage metrics, outcomes delivered, and a 12-month value roadmap.

retrievedKnowledge.title: Low Adoption Intervention Playbook
retrievedKnowledge.content: If product adoption falls below 50%, schedule an Adoption Workshop within 14 days. Identify the top 3 unused features relevant to the customer's use case. Assign a Solutions Engineer to run a hands-on training session. Follow up with a 30-day adoption improvement plan and weekly check-ins. Measure adoption weekly post-workshop.

retrievedKnowledge.title: Customer Health Data Collection Playbook
retrievedKnowledge.content: If adoption or NPS data is unavailable, immediately request access to product analytics dashboards. Schedule a data-sharing onboarding session. Send a CSAT survey via email within 3 days. Set up automated health score tracking in your CRM. Without health data, you are flying blind - this is a P1 task.

If product adoption falls below 50%, schedule an Adoption Workshop within 14 days. Identify the top 3 unused features relevant to the customer's use case. Assign a Solutions Engineer to run a hands-on training session. Follow up with a 30-day adoption improvement plan and weekly check-ins. Measure adoption weekly post-workshop.

If adoption or NPS data is unavailable, immediately request access to product analytics dashboards. Schedule a data-sharing onboarding session. Send a CSAT survey via email within 3 days. Set up automated health score tracking in your CRM. Without health data, you are flying blind - this is a P1 task.

scenarios.name: Executive Business Review
scenarios.summary: Bring executives together to validate the renewal plan and address churn risk.

scenarios.name: Quarterly Payment Plan
scenarios.summary: Offer a flexible payment schedule to balance cash flow and retention.

scenarios.name: 20% Discount
scenarios.summary: Use a price incentive to improve renewal likelihood while accepting margin pressure.

scenarios.name: Additional Product Training
scenarios.summary: Improve adoption through targeted training to increase engagement and retention.

scenarios.name: No Action
scenarios.summary: Maintain the status quo and do not intervene aggressively.

bestScenario.name: Additional Product Training
bestScenario.summary: Improve adoption through targeted training to increase engagement and retention.

potentialWeaknesses: Potential revenue impact from the selected option.
The selected scenario may create customer expectations that are hard to sustain.
longTermDisadvantages: Margin pressure could harm future renewal negotiations.
It may reduce ability to pursue higher-value upsell opportunities.
revenueTradeOffs: Short-term renewal may come at the cost of long-term revenue growth.
customerRelationshipRisks: Customers may expect similar concessions in the next cycle.

alternative.name: Quarterly Payment Plan
alternative.summary: A balanced approach that preserves revenue while improving renewal probability.

devilsAdvocateReview.finalVerdict: The selected scenario should be reviewed for risk; a more balanced alternative may be preferable.

recommendations.action: Escalate to Senior Customer Success Manager
